codeReview.summary: The feature adds broad automated coverage for local CLI first-run behavior, but it does not fully satisfy the install-matrix requirement because the Homebrew path is validated only by formula text assertions rather than an executable install/run flow.

issues.description: VAL-INSTALL-007 requires both npm and Homebrew distribution paths to produce a working CLI entrypoint, but the Homebrew section only parses/regex-checks Formula/mors.rb (e.g., lines 120-174 and 186-196) and never executes a brewed mors command. This can pass even when real Homebrew install/runtime behavior is broken.

Install-guidance assertions are shallow string checks (README contains 'MORS_CONFIG_DIR', 'node dist/index.js', etc.) and do not verify that user-facing install guidance for distribution channels maps to actual executable paths/commands end-to-end.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared user-testing guidance is heavily centered on `node dist/index.js` execution and does not document a standard way to capture npm/global and Homebrew install-path evidence for install-UX validations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/user-testing.md (Flow Validator Guidance) prescribes `MORS_CONFIG_DIR=... node dist/index.js <command>` and says CLI is pre-built, with no corresponding guidance for validating installed `mors` entrypoints from npm/Homebrew.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill mandates strict red/green TDD but lacks explicit handling guidance for validation-only features where new tests may intentionally pass against existing behavior without source changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mors-worker/SKILL.md step 2 requires strict TDD; transcript skeleton for session d09eb505-3cf2-41d8-b4cd-6ec7250dec87 shows `test/install-matrix.test.ts` was created and passed on first run with no production-code iteration.

summary: Review failed: commit 3b43eca adds substantial first-run/install checks, but Homebrew validation is not end-to-end executable and therefore does not fully satisfy the install-matrix requirement for VAL-INSTALL-007.
